prompts.title: Literature Review Theme Mapping
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PhD methodologist with 15 years guiding scholars at IITs and JNU. I will paste 25 abstracts from my reading list. Map them into a thematic synthesis table with columns: theme cluster, sub-themes, contributing authors, methodological approach (qualitative/quantitative/mixed), key findings, contradictions vs other studies, and the research gap implied. Then propose 3 candidate research questions emerging from the thematic gaps, each phrased to satisfy the IIS PhD synopsis convention (1 main question + 3 sub-questions). Format as a comparison matrix + RQ proposal. Constraints: avoid quoting more than 7 words from any abstract; classify approach using common social-science taxonomies; flag any paper that contradicts the established narrative.

prompts.title: Research Gap Statement (PhD Synopsis)
prompts.prompt: Act as a PhD supervisor at JNU with 12 years guiding social science scholars. Help me articulate the research gap statement for my synopsis on 'Digital labour rights for Indian gig economy workers'. I will paste my literature review section and 12 key citations. Produce: (1) the dominant narrative in current literature (90 words), (2) 3 specific gaps (theoretical, empirical, contextual), (3) why each gap matters for Indian policy or worker welfare, (4) 1 paragraph (180 words) that connects my proposed work to filling these gaps, (5) 4 candidate originality contributions. Format as a synopsis-ready section with citation placeholders. Constraints: align with UGC PhD ordinance 2022; keep claims defensible; avoid grand-claim words like 'first ever' or 'revolutionary'.

prompts.title: Methodology Section for Mixed-Methods Study
prompts.prompt: Act as a methods professor with 18 years teaching mixed-methods research across IIM and TISS. Draft my methodology section for a PhD on 'Adoption of UPI in tier-3 Indian cities'. I'll do quant survey (n=600, stratified random sampling across 6 cities) + qual interviews (n=30, purposive). Section should cover: (1) ontological + epistemological position (pragmatism), (2) research design (sequential explanatory mixed-methods), (3) sampling logic and selection criteria, (4) instruments (translated from English to Hindi/regional), (5) data collection plan (8 months, 6 cities, ASHA-worker liaisons), (6) analysis approach (SEM in AMOS for quant, thematic analysis in NVivo for qual), (7) integration logic, (8) ethics (DPDP, IIT IRB, informed consent in regional language), (9) trustworthiness (validity/reliability + credibility). Format as a thesis chapter section. Constraints: cite Creswell & Plano Clark; respect Indian regulatory norms for human-subject research.

prompts.title: Doctoral Thesis Chapter Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior thesis advisor at IIT Madras with 14 years across PhD viva voces. I'm writing my thesis: 'Machine learning for predictive maintenance in Indian railway rolling stock'. Build me the full chapter-by-chapter outline. For each of 8 chapters provide: (1) chapter title, (2) chapter aim (one sentence), (3) sub-section headings, (4) approximate word count, (5) figures + tables I should include, (6) interaction with my 4 research questions (RQ1-RQ4), (7) which methodology element is anchored in this chapter, (8) which paper publishability slice maps to this chapter. Format as a tabular thesis blueprint + a writing-order recommendation (which to write first). Constraints: align with UGC PhD ordinance 2022 + IIT Madras format; total 80,000-90,000 words; English style guide; minimum 200 references; original contributions clearly demarcated.

prompts.title: Ethics Application for Human-Subjects Research
prompts.prompt: Act as a research ethics officer with 9 years at IIT Bombay IRB. Draft my ethics application for a PhD study involving 50 in-depth interviews with Indian women MSME entrepreneurs. Application must cover: (1) study purpose + benefits (society, participants), (2) recruitment (snowball + purposive, no coercion), (3) informed consent (Hindi/Marathi/English, written + verbal options), (4) risk assessment (low — psychological + reputational), (5) data handling (anonymisation, encryption at rest, 5-year retention, deletion thereafter — DPDP 2023 compliant), (6) participant withdrawal rights (any time, no penalty, data deletion within 14 days), (7) compensation (₹500 voucher — disclosed in consent form), (8) conflict of interest disclosure, (9) data sharing for replication (anonymised dataset on Mendeley after thesis submission), (10) named PI + Co-PI + supervisor. Format as a 4-page IRB form. Constraints: align with ICMR ethics code 2017; DPDP Act compliance; supervisor sign-off section.

prompts.title: PhD Comprehensive Exam Question Bank
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior faculty at IISc Bengaluru, electrical communications engineering. Design a 24-question comprehensive exam question bank for a 2nd-year PhD scholar in 5G/6G wireless communications. Cover: (1) 8 fundamental theory questions (information theory, channel coding, MIMO, OFDM), (2) 8 advanced research questions (URLLC, mMTC, NOMA, IRS-assisted), (3) 8 critical-thinking questions (paper critique, design trade-off, real-world deployment for Indian rural 5G). For each: (a) question text (50 words), (b) expected answer length (5/15/30 min), (c) marking rubric (4 levels), (d) seminal references the scholar should cite, (e) common-mistake flag. Format as a structured exam bank. Constraints: align with IISc course ordinance; questions must integrate Indian context (Reliance Jio, BSNL, IIT 5G testbed); anchored in PhD-level rigour, not master's-level.

prompts.title: Statistical Power Analysis Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a biostatistician with 12 years at AIIMS / PGIMER advising MD/PhD scholars. I'm running a randomized controlled trial of a behavioural intervention for diabetes self-management among Indian patients (target effect size: HbA1c reduction by 0.5%, SD 1.2%, two-sided alpha 0.05, power 80%). Compute: (1) required sample size per arm using G*Power (with formula), (2) attrition adjustment (15% over 12 months), (3) cluster correction if I use a community-based design (ICC 0.05), (4) multi-site stratification rationale, (5) interim analysis plan (one at 50% recruitment), (6) handling missing data (multiple imputation), (7) sensitivity analysis. Format as a power-analysis plan document. Constraints: align with ICMR clinical trial registry CTRI; cite seminal references (Cohen, Borm); account for Indian rural/urban heterogeneity; pre-register on CTRI before recruitment.

prompts.title: Research Conference Abstract (300 Words)
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior researcher with 10 years of publishing in IEEE/ACM/Nature Indian-author conferences. Write a 300-word conference abstract for my PhD work on 'Federated learning for Indian agritech crop disease detection'. Structure: (1) 1-line problem statement (40 words: contextual urgency), (2) approach (60 words: federated CNN with privacy-preserving aggregation, 12 farmer cohorts across Karnataka and Maharashtra), (3) data (40 words: 28K leaf images, 6 disease categories, 4 crops), (4) results (60 words: F1, precision, recall vs centralized baseline; communication cost), (5) novelty (40 words: cross-cohort heterogeneity handling), (6) implications (40 words: scalable to NAFED + ICAR-KVK extension), (7) keyword set (8 terms). Format as a publication-ready abstract. Constraints: avoid jargon-overload; quantify every claim; respect target conference word limit; first sentence must hook the reviewer.

prompts.title: Viva-Voce Mock Question Set
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior external examiner with 16 years on Indian PhD viva panels (UGC-funded universities). Generate a 25-question mock viva for a PhD scholar defending a thesis on 'Solar mini-grids in rural Madhya Pradesh: techno-economic feasibility'. Cover: (1) 5 questions on theoretical framing, (2) 5 on methodology (techno-economic analysis tools, sensitivity analysis), (3) 5 on data + results interpretation, (4) 5 on policy/practical implications, (5) 5 on originality + future work. For each: (a) question text, (b) expected answer key (3 bullets), (c) common pitfall the scholar should avoid, (d) follow-up probing question. Format as a viva preparation document. Constraints: hard but fair questions consistent with Indian PhD viva norms; align with UGC PhD ordinance 2022 evaluation criteria; encourage critical defence not memorisation.

prompts.title: Plagiarism Audit Pre-Submission
prompts.prompt: Act as a research integrity officer at IIT Delhi with 11 years of pre-submission plagiarism audits. Audit my thesis (90,000 words) using Turnitin / Ouriginal logic. Provide: (1) interpretation guidance for Turnitin similarity index by section (intro 22%, lit review 32%, methods 28%, results 12%, discussion 18%, references variable), (2) acceptable vs problematic flags (paraphrase too close, citation missing, self-plagiarism from my own conference paper, common-knowledge phrases), (3) section-wise remediation steps, (4) self-citation rules per UGC ordinance, (5) figures + tables permission audit (CC-BY vs publisher-permission), (6) AI-generated content disclosure (UGC 2024 advisory), (7) citation style consistency check (APA 7 / IEEE / IFAC), (8) final declaration form text. Format as an audit report + remediation tracker. Constraints: align with UGC PhD ordinance 2022 + AI disclosure norms; preserve original voice; flag every borderline section, do not auto-rewrite.

prompts.title: Conference Travel Grant Application
prompts.prompt: Act as a research office grant manager. Help me apply for a SERB / DST International Travel Support (ITS) grant to attend NeurIPS 2026 (Vancouver) where my paper is accepted. Application should include: (1) 250-word abstract of accepted paper, (2) 200-word justification why this conference is critical to my PhD, (3) detailed travel budget in INR (flight, accommodation, registration, daily allowance — DST norms), (4) supervisor endorsement letter draft (180 words), (5) departmental endorsement template, (6) post-conference deliverable plan (seminar, blog post, knowledge transfer to lab), (7) co-funding declaration (CSIR / institute), (8) named bank account + PAN for disbursement. Format as a complete application packet. Constraints: align with SERB ITS scheme guidelines; compete with 1,200 applicants typically; honest INR estimates; thank-you letter to SERB on return.

prompts.title: PhD Progress Report (Annual DC Meeting)
prompts.prompt: Act as a doctoral committee (DC) chair at NIT Trichy. Draft a comprehensive annual progress report for a 3rd-year PhD scholar in mechanical engineering working on 'Topology optimization for additive manufacturing in Indian aerospace components'. Report should cover: (1) RQ progress against original timeline, (2) deliverables completed (papers submitted/accepted, code repos, datasets, tools), (3) coursework + comprehensive exam status, (4) industry collaboration progress (HAL Bangalore, ISRO Vikram Sarabhai), (5) gaps and risks (e.g., delay in machine access at central facility), (6) mitigation plan for each risk, (7) revised timeline for next 12 months with milestones, (8) DC member feedback (reserved spaces), (9) scholarship continuation recommendation. Format as an A4 progress report with signature blocks. Constraints: align with NIT Trichy PhD ordinance; integrate INSA / SERB grant reporting requirements where relevant; honest about delays.

prompts.title: Cold Email to International Collaborator
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PI at an Indian research lab with 8 years of building international collaborations. Draft a 220-word cold email from a PhD scholar to a Stanford / MIT / ETH Zurich professor proposing a research collaboration. The PhD is at IIT Kharagpur on 'Climate-resilient maize varieties using gene editing in Indian smallholder context'. Email should: (1) reference one recent paper of theirs (specific, not generic flattery), (2) introduce the scholar's lab + supervisor (1 line), (3) propose a 6-month visiting scholar stint or co-supervision arrangement, (4) outline 3 mutual benefits (data complementarity, methodology exchange, co-authorship potential), (5) mention available funding source (DBT-Wellcome Trust India Alliance), (6) attach a 2-page concept note (mention only), (7) request a 30-min Zoom slot. Format as a ready-to-send email + concept note outline. Constraints: respectful, not deferential; specific not vague; cite at least 2 of their papers in concept note.

prompts.title: Citation Manager Setup for PhD Workflow
prompts.prompt: Act as an academic productivity coach. I'm a 1st-year PhD scholar at JNU. Set up my full Zotero workflow for the next 5 years. Cover: (1) account + library structure (collections + subcollections by RQ + lit-review themes), (2) browser connector + auto-import rules, (3) PDF storage strategy (cloud + local backup + university repository), (4) citation style installation (APA 7 + Chicago + my journal-specific), (5) MS Word + Google Docs plugin setup, (6) shared library with my supervisor + co-scholars, (7) tagging system (read/skim/cite/quote, theme tags, methodology tags), (8) integration with Notion for thesis chapter outlines, (9) annotation discipline (highlight + note + question prompt), (10) backup + version-control routine (weekly export to ORCID profile). Format as a setup playbook with screenshots-to-add markers. Constraints: free tier where possible (₹0 spend); DPDP-compliant cloud storage (preferring institutional); workflow scales to 600 citations.

prompts.title: Pre-Submission Paper Critique
prompts.prompt: Act as a critical peer reviewer with 14 years of reviewing for IEEE TPAMI and ACM TOIS. Critique a draft research paper before I submit. I'll paste the manuscript. Provide: (1) 5-bullet executive summary (what works, what's weak), (2) section-by-section critique (intro, related work, method, experiments, results, discussion, conclusion) with concrete suggestions, (3) novelty assessment (what's original vs incremental), (4) experimental rigour audit (baseline coverage, ablation, statistical significance, error bars), (5) writing clarity flags (10 most ambiguous sentences), (6) reviewer-magnet pitfalls (claims unsupported, missing related work, overclaim), (7) figure + table audit, (8) revision priority list (top 5). Format as a reviewer report with line-anchored comments. Constraints: be critical but constructive; cite alternative methods I might have missed; avoid 'reject for sport'; mock referee scores (1-10) on novelty + soundness + clarity.

prompts.title: Dataset Release with FAIR Principles
prompts.prompt: Act as a research data manager at TIFR Mumbai with 9 years of dataset publication. Help me release my PhD dataset (30 GB Indian air-quality sensor data, 18 months, 6 cities) following FAIR principles. Provide: (1) license recommendation (CC BY 4.0 vs CDLA), (2) repository selection (Zenodo / Mendeley / Indian institutional — pros & cons), (3) DOI registration steps, (4) metadata schema (Dublin Core + DCAT), (5) README structure (15 sections), (6) data dictionary template (column-by-column), (7) sample loading code (Python + R), (8) versioning policy (semantic versioning), (9) ethics + DPDP audit (no PII), (10) citation snippet (BibTeX + APA), (11) preservation plan (10-year retention). Format as a dataset publication checklist. Constraints: align with NDLI / ShodhGanga + UGC open-access policy; FAIR-aligned per GO-FAIR India initiative; include Indian language metadata fallback (Hindi optional).

prompts.title: Pre-Doc Submission Cover Letter
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PI at an Indian research institute. Write a 280-word post-doc application cover letter from a PhD scholar in computational biology to apply for a position at Christian Medical College Vellore in their genomics lab. Letter should: (1) open by referencing a specific recent paper from the host lab + the scholar's interest in it, (2) summarise PhD focus + 3 most relevant skills, (3) propose a specific research direction the scholar could pursue (1 paragraph), (4) mention complementary expertise the scholar brings (e.g., novel computational pipeline + clinical sample integration), (5) confirm fit with the lab's clinical-translational orientation, (6) request a Zoom interview, (7) attach CV + 2 most-relevant papers + 3 references. Provide companion 1-page CV outline. Format as a ready-to-send cover letter + CV scaffold. Constraints: avoid generic 'I am passionate about'; quantify achievements; respect Indian institutional conventions.

prompts.title: Writing Routine for PhD Productivity
prompts.prompt: Act as a productivity coach with 8 years working with Indian PhD scholars across IITs and IIMs. Design a sustainable 12-month writing routine for a 3rd-year scholar trying to finish their thesis (estimated 60K words remaining) plus 2 journal papers. Cover: (1) weekly architecture (deep-write blocks, edit blocks, reading blocks, supervisor meetings), (2) daily structure (4 hour-long pomodoro blocks, energy-aligned), (3) writing accountability (daily word count target 800; weekly review with peer), (4) tooling stack (Scrivener / Word / Overleaf, Zotero, Notion, Forest app), (5) failure recovery (off-day protocol, perfectionism block, supervisor reschedule), (6) life-balance (1 day off / month off, exercise, sleep), (7) monthly review template, (8) milestone celebration (a thali after each chapter complete). Format as a writing-routine playbook. Constraints: realistic for a scholar with 8K HRA stipend; respect Indian family-life context; avoid hustle-culture clichés.

prompts.title: Thesis Acknowledgments Section
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior thesis editor with 10 years across IIT and IIM thesis manuscripts. Write a 600-word thesis acknowledgments section for a PhD scholar at IIM Ahmedabad whose work was on 'Behavioural finance among Indian gig workers'. Section should thank in this order: (1) supervisor (warm, specific anecdote — 90 words), (2) co-supervisor (60 words), (3) doctoral committee (60 words), (4) UGC-CSIR JRF/SRF funding source (40 words), (5) data collaborators (Razorpay + Urban Company HR — 60 words), (6) participants (the 1,200 gig workers — 80 words), (7) lab mates / cohort (60 words), (8) family (parents, spouse — 90 words), (9) personal — friends, gym buddies, the chai-wallah (40 words), (10) closing reflection (20 words). Format as a thesis-ready acknowledgments section. Constraints: warm but not saccharine; mention only people who genuinely helped (no obligatory thanks); include one specific moment per major thank-you to make it human.

prompts.title: Open Science Pre-Registration Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as an open-science advocate at an Indian research institute (similar to IndiaRxiv / OSF India). Draft a pre-registration plan on OSF for a PhD experiment: 'Effect of UPI cashback nudges on saving behaviour of Indian millennials'. Plan should cover: (1) hypothesis (directional + null), (2) sample size + recruitment (calculated via power analysis, n=720), (3) experimental design (3-arm RCT with 12-week follow-up), (4) measurement instruments (validated scales — DOSE), (5) statistical plan (ANCOVA with covariates), (6) exclusion criteria (a priori), (7) data analysis pipeline (R scripts pre-shared), (8) timestamp + DOI, (9) deviation reporting protocol, (10) publication commitment (results regardless of significance). Format as an OSF-ready pre-registration. Constraints: align with Open Science Framework + UGC's growing open-access posture; protect against HARKing + p-hacking; commit to data + code sharing post-publication.
